publicSideBand: Public-side work is being funded through Bloomington's water capital program
privateSideBand: The city anticipates customer-side replacement costs beginning in 2027 will be covered by rate increases with no additional cost to the owner
fullReplacementBand: Full replacement is expected to be no additional cost under the current rate-funded plan but the city says final customer costs are not guaranteed
housingTypeAssumption: Bloomington publishes a planned citywide funding approach rather than contractor bid ranges
permitAssumption: Final customer responsibility depends on available funds and the block-by-block project schedule
restorationAssumption: Restoration and final scope will follow the city-managed construction process
methodologySummary: Bloomington does not publish contractor bid ranges but it does publish a utility-specific rate-funded replacement plan where recent water rate increases are intended to cover block-by-block work beginning in 2027 while the city still keeps a caveat on final customer-side coverage.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners avoid direct cost only when Bloomington keeps the address inside the scheduled rate-funded city replacement path and available funds hold rather than leaving the property on an owner-managed private replacement outside the block program.
